justification: All pass criteria met: 5 services recommended with strong justification for why not 6 (Customer merged with Order due to cross-service joins); Location Tracking and Payment both correctly identified as separation candidates with specific NFR reasoning (5K writes/sec vs ACID consistency/PCI); team size constraint explicitly factored throughout (1.5 engineers per service, phased migration to avoid operational overload); Strangler Fig migration pattern recommended with detailed 4-phase sequence; trade-offs include operational overhead and distributed tracing complexity; risks explicitly cover distributed transactions between Order and Payment.

observations.text: Excellent justification for 5 services instead of 6 — correctly identifies that Customer Account Management creates cross-service joins on every order read if separated
Location Tracking isolation is well-justified by 5K writes/sec requiring different storage (Redis+PostGIS) and CQRS pattern
Payment isolation justified by both consistency requirements and PCI compliance boundary
Strangler Fig migration is detailed with a 4-phase sequence ordered by extraction difficulty and coupling
Distributed transaction risk explicitly called out with concrete mitigation (idempotency keys, synchronous call, compensation refund)
Operational overhead risk rated Critical with phased mitigation — shows awareness of team size constraint
Team skill gap acknowledged as a risk with concrete mitigation (game days, observability investment)
Sync vs async Payment call trade-off is well-reasoned — rejects saga pattern due to team maturity
Recommends 5 services rather than the ideal 4 — Notification Service could arguably stay in the monolith longer given team size, though the justification for separation is reasonable
Anti-corruption layer and dual-run validation steps show mature migration thinking
Pitfalls section adds practical value beyond the rubric requirements — shared databases, premature sagas, event enrichment

agent_output_excerpt:
## Executive Summary

Decompose the monolith into **5 services** aligned to domain boundaries, not 6 — Customer Account Management should merge with Order Management as a supporting concern, not a standalone service. The critical architectural decision is isolating the high-throughput Location Tracking service (5K writes/sec) from the consistency-critical Payment service, while keeping the decomposition small enough for an 8-person team to actually operate. A Strangler Fig migration over 3-4 pha
